title: Tefillin Explained: The Black Boxes That Connect Us to God
title_zh: 经文匣深度解析：连接我们与上帝的黑色盒子
title_he: תפילין מוסברות: הקופסאות השחורות שמחברות אותנו לאלוהים
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
chinese_parallels: unity of heaven and humanity (tian ren he yi)
target_audience: Readers with basic knowledge seeking deeper understanding
cultural_context: Chinese readers familiar with spiritual practices like qigong and meditation
historical_period: Biblical period through modern Chassidic thought
chinese_dynasty_reference: Late Shang Dynasty (商朝晚期, ~1300 BCE), Early Han Dynasty (漢朝早期)
header_image_description: Traditional Chinese ink wash painting depicting a misty mountain peak at dawn with ethereal golden light breaking through clouds. In the foreground, two ancient stone pillars stand on either side of a winding path leading upward, with subtle Hebrew letters (shin-dalet-yud) formed naturally in the rock formations. The composition balances earthly and heavenly realms, painted in deep blacks, soft grays, and luminous gold accents, conveying spiritual connection between the physical and divine. Style: classical shan shui with Taoist philosophical undertones.
mystical_concepts: Four spiritual worlds (Atzilut, Beriah, Yetzirah, Asiyah)
